text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little village nestled between emerald forests and shimmering lakes, there lived a wise old owl named Owsla. Owsla had an idea so big it could never quite take flight. Every evening, she’d dream of a grand adventure across the vast oceans, soaring over oceans of golden seashells and mountains made of twinkling starberries.

One quiet autumn morning, Owsla decided to act on her dream. Gathering every feathered companion she could find, including a lonely golden plover, she set out on her journey.

Their first trip was across the Great Ocean Blue, where the waves sang histories only feathers could understand. Owsla learned to talk to the sea creatures, telling tales of the owls she had met. They sailed further, visiting islands with forests made of coral that whispered stories only Owsla could hear.
